scenario: returns prod when pathname does not start with /demo
whyItMatters: All non-demo routes must talk to the production Supabase instance.
risk: Wrong env resolution would send prod admin data to the demo DB.

scenario: returns demo when pathname starts with /demo
whyItMatters: Demo routes must be isolated from the production database.
risk: Resolving demo as prod would let demo users read or write production data.

scenario: returns prod when window is undefined (SSR / Node context)
whyItMatters: Server-side rendering or Node test runs must not crash on missing window.
risk: A window check missing would throw ReferenceError in every Node-side unit test.

scenario: isDemoEnvironment returns true only when pathname starts with /demo
whyItMatters: Convenience boolean used across hooks and components to gate demo-only UI.
risk: Wrong return value would show demo banners in prod or hide them in demo.

scenario: returns prod when pathname is /demo-settings (starts with /demo but not a demo route)
whyItMatters: Paths like /demo-settings are prod admin routes, not demo namespace — must not be misclassified.
risk: Prefix-only matching would treat /demo-settings as demo, routing admin calls to the wrong DB.

scenario: cn merges multiple class strings into one
whyItMatters: cn is used throughout the UI to build conditional className strings.
risk: Broken merging would corrupt component styling across the entire app.

scenario: cn ignores undefined, null, and false values
whyItMatters: Conditional classes must not inject 'undefined' or 'false' strings into the DOM.
risk: Falsy strings leaking into className break CSS selectors and introduce visual noise.

scenario: formatDateTime returns em dash for null/undefined input
whyItMatters: Score and audit timestamps may be null; UI must show a dash, not 'Invalid Date'.
risk: Missing guard would render 'Invalid Date' or throw in every table cell with no timestamp.

scenario: formatDateTime returns a non-empty string for a valid ISO timestamp
whyItMatters: Timestamps must be human-readable in the admin audit log and score grid.
risk: A broken formatter would display raw ISO strings or empty strings in the UI.

scenario: formatDate returns em dash for null/undefined input
whyItMatters: Period dates may be null before an evaluation period is configured.
risk: Missing guard would crash the Periods table render.

scenario: formatDate returns a date-only string without time components
whyItMatters: Period pages show date-only values; time components must be excluded.
risk: Time leaking into date-only fields would create visual clutter.

scenario: formatTime returns em dash for null/undefined input
whyItMatters: Time-only display used in session logs; null inputs must not crash.
risk: Missing guard throws in session detail views.

scenario: randomUUID delegates to crypto.randomUUID when available
whyItMatters: Secure contexts (https/localhost) must use the native crypto API for UUIDs.
risk: Ignoring the native API would use the weaker Math.random fallback unnecessarily.

scenario: randomUUID returns a valid UUID v4 string when crypto.randomUUID is unavailable
whyItMatters: QR scan on local http:// does not have crypto.randomUUID — correlation IDs must still work.
risk: No fallback would throw and break the entire jury entry flow on non-secure origins.

scenario: randomUUID returns distinct values on consecutive calls
whyItMatters: Correlation IDs must be unique per request to avoid log collisions.
risk: Colliding IDs would make tracing individual requests in logs impossible.

scenario: DEMO_MODE is false when loaded outside the /demo path
whyItMatters: DEMO_MODE gates demo-specific UI and data; wrong value in prod would break production admin.
risk: DEMO_MODE=true in prod would show demo banners to real users and may suppress real auth flows.

scenario: DEMO_MODE is true when loaded on the /demo path
whyItMatters: Demo auto-login and demo banners must activate on the /demo namespace.
risk: DEMO_MODE=false in demo would prevent the auto-login loader from running.

scenario: calling supabase.rpc() invokes the underlying client's rpc method
whyItMatters: All API calls go through the Proxy; broken forwarding would silence every DB call.
risk: Proxy not forwarding would cause silent null returns from every RPC across the app.

scenario: supabase methods retain correct `this` binding when called through the Proxy
whyItMatters: Supabase client methods depend on `this` for internal state; unbound calls would throw.
risk: Lost binding causes TypeError in any RPC/query call.

scenario: clearPersistedSession does not throw when localStorage is unavailable
whyItMatters: Safari private mode and full storage can make localStorage.removeItem throw.
risk: An uncaught error in clearPersistedSession would break the sign-out flow.

scenario: dbScoresToUi maps written to design and oral to delivery
whyItMatters: Every score read from the DB must surface under the correct UI field names.
risk: Wrong mapping would display design scores under delivery and vice versa in the admin grid.

scenario: dbScoresToUi returns null for missing fields
whyItMatters: Null scores must stay null, not become 0 or undefined.
risk: Coercing null to 0 would inflate zero-score averages.

scenario: uiScoresToDb maps design to written and delivery to oral
whyItMatters: Score writes must target the correct DB columns.
risk: Wrong column names would write scores to non-existent or wrong columns, silently losing data.

scenario: uiScoresToDb returns null for missing UI fields
whyItMatters: Partial score saves must preserve null, not write 0.
risk: Coercing null to 0 would overwrite absent scores with zeros on every autosave.

scenario: dbAvgScoresToUi converts string-typed averages to Numbers
whyItMatters: Postgres NUMERIC columns arrive as strings; analytics charts need JS Numbers.
risk: String values passed to chart libraries render as NaN bars.

scenario: dbAvgScoresToUi returns null when DB avg is null
whyItMatters: A period with no scores has null averages; charts must render empty, not 0.
risk: Null→0 coercion would show spurious zero bars in trend charts.

scenario: formatMembers joins an array of {name} objects to a semicolon-separated string
whyItMatters: Project member lists stored as JSONB arrays must render as readable strings.
risk: Broken formatting would display '[object Object]' in the score grid.

scenario: formatMembers converts a comma-separated string to semicolons
whyItMatters: Legacy imports stored members as comma-delimited strings.
risk: Comma-delimited output in a semicolon-delimited column breaks CSV exports.

scenario: formatMembers returns empty string for null, undefined, or empty input
whyItMatters: Projects with no members are valid; UI must not crash.
risk: Calling .split() on null throws TypeError and crashes the score grid row.

scenario: uiScoresToDb keeps technical and teamwork field names unchanged
whyItMatters: These two fields have the same name in DB and UI; any rename would break them.
risk: Silently renaming technical or teamwork would drop those scores on write.

scenario: A DB row that goes through dbScoresToUi then uiScoresToDb is identical to the original
whyItMatters: Read-then-write must be lossless; any field rename applied twice would corrupt data.
risk: Double-mapping would overwrite correct scores with nulls on the next autosave.

scenario: invokeEdgeFunction returns { data, error: null } on 200 response
whyItMatters: Every Edge Function call goes through this wrapper; success path must work.
risk: Broken success path would surface errors in maintenance, export, and pin-reset flows.

scenario: invokeEdgeFunction returns { data: null, error } on non-2xx status
whyItMatters: Edge Function failures must propagate as errors, not silently return null data.
risk: Swallowing errors would make admin actions appear to succeed when the function actually failed.

story: 401 retry — refreshes session and retries once
scenario: invokeEdgeFunction retries with refreshed token on 401
whyItMatters: Expired tokens during long admin sessions must be transparently refreshed.
risk: No retry would force admins to manually reload after token expiry mid-workflow.

scenario: withRetry returns immediately on first success
whyItMatters: The happy path must not add latency from unnecessary retry logic.
risk: Extra retry iterations on success would slow every DB call.

scenario: withRetry retries up to maxAttempts on TypeError (network failure)
whyItMatters: Transient network blips must not fail score saves during evaluation day.
risk: No retry would lose juror score submissions on any brief network interruption.

scenario: withRetry propagates AbortError immediately without retrying
whyItMatters: Intentional cancellations (page navigation) must not trigger retry delays.
risk: Retrying after abort would run 3 DB calls after every page unload.

story: does not retry on business error
scenario: withRetry propagates non-network errors immediately
whyItMatters: Auth failures and constraint violations must surface immediately, not after 3 retries.
risk: Retrying auth errors would triple login-failure audit events.

scenario: authenticateJuror calls rpc_jury_authenticate with trimmed name and affiliation
whyItMatters: The RPC must receive the exact parameter shape or the DB function rejects the call.
risk: Wrong param names or untrimmed whitespace would cause auth to fail for jurors.

scenario: authenticateJuror throws when supabase.rpc returns an error
whyItMatters: DB errors must surface so the UI can show an error state.
risk: Swallowing errors would leave jurors stuck at the authentication step with no feedback.

story: listPeriods — returns empty array on null data
scenario: listPeriods returns [] when supabase returns null data
whyItMatters: Jury period selection must render an empty list, not crash on null.
risk: Returning null would cause .map() crash in the period selection step.

scenario: upsertScore maps session_expired error_code to a named error
whyItMatters: Session expiry during scoring must trigger the correct UI recovery flow.
risk: Unmapped error codes would silently swallow the session expiry, leaving jurors unable to save.

story: getSession — returns null when no user
scenario: getSession returns null when supabase.auth.getUser returns no user
whyItMatters: Unauthenticated admin visits must get null, not crash or return stale data.
risk: Non-null return for unauthenticated user would bypass the PendingReviewGate.

scenario: checkEmailAvailable calls rpc_check_email_available with p_email
whyItMatters: Registration validation depends on this RPC; wrong param name would always return available.
risk: Wrong param would allow duplicate registrations.

scenario: listOrganizationsPublic throws when supabase returns an error
whyItMatters: Registration org dropdown must surface DB errors.
risk: Swallowing error would show an empty dropdown with no feedback.

scenario: upsertProfile throws 'Not authenticated' when no user session
whyItMatters: Profile saves must require a valid session.
risk: Silent failure would lose display name changes without user feedback.

story: getProfile — returns null when no user
scenario: getProfile returns null when supabase.auth.getUser returns no user
whyItMatters: Profile fetch on unauthenticated visit must return null, not throw.
risk: Throwing would crash the settings page for logged-out users.

scenario: updateJuror throws when called without an id
whyItMatters: Missing ID would silently update all juror rows.
risk: Silent bulk update would corrupt the entire juror list.

scenario: setJurorEditMode throws when jurorId or periodId is missing
whyItMatters: Edit mode toggle without IDs would run an unbounded RPC.
risk: Missing IDs would toggle edit mode for the wrong juror or all jurors.

scenario: resetJurorPin calls rpc_juror_reset_pin with p_period_id and p_juror_id
whyItMatters: Pin reset RPC must receive the correct parameter shape.
risk: Wrong param names would silently no-op, leaving jurors locked out.

scenario: createPeriod returns created period data on success
whyItMatters: Admin must receive the new period's ID immediately for downstream operations.
risk: Not returning data would force a page reload to get the new period ID.

scenario: updatePeriod throws when called without an id
whyItMatters: Missing ID guard prevents silently patching all periods.
risk: Updating without ID would overwrite every period in the org.

scenario: getPeriodMaxScore returns null when no criteria exist
whyItMatters: Score grid must show 'not configured' state, not 0/100, for unconfigured periods.
risk: Returning 0 as max score would divide-by-zero in percentage calculations.

scenario: getDeleteCounts returns projects, scores, and jurorAssignments for period target
whyItMatters: Delete confirmation modal must show accurate cascade counts.
risk: Wrong counts would mislead admins about the impact of a delete operation.

scenario: deleteEntity throws 'Unsupported delete target.' for unknown targetType
whyItMatters: Unknown target types must fail loudly before touching the DB.
risk: Silent no-op would make admins think a delete succeeded when it didn't.

scenario: getActiveEntryTokenPlain returns null when token is expired
whyItMatters: Expired tokens must not be handed to jurors.
risk: Returning an expired token would let jurors attempt to use it and get cryptic errors.

scenario: revokeEntryToken returns { success, active_juror_count, revoked_count }
whyItMatters: UI shows a confirmation with affected counts after revoke.
risk: Missing counts would render blank confirmation modal.

story: logExportInitiated — throws on invalid action prefix
scenario: logExportInitiated throws when action does not start with 'export.'
whyItMatters: Audit integrity requires all export events to have the correct action prefix.
risk: Wrong prefix would make exports invisible in the audit log export filter.

scenario: writeAuthFailureEvent does not throw when supabase.rpc returns an error
whyItMatters: Auth failure audit writes must not block the login UI.
risk: Throwing here would crash the login page on every failed login attempt.

scenario: writeAuditLog calls rpc_admin_write_audit_event with a p_event object
whyItMatters: Audit log writes depend on the correct RPC and param shape.
risk: Wrong param name would silently no-op all client-side audit writes.

scenario: KEYS exports all expected keys for jury flow, admin, and shared UI
whyItMatters: Missing keys would cause hardcoded fallback strings to creep back in.
risk: Hardcoded strings scattered across components break when a key changes.

scenario: All KEYS values are unique strings
whyItMatters: Duplicate storage keys cause two modules to share a storage slot and overwrite each other.
risk: Key collision would corrupt jury session data with admin filter state.

scenario: setJuryAccess writes to both sessionStorage and localStorage; getJuryAccess reads from either
whyItMatters: Jury access must survive tab close (localStorage) and be isolated per tab (sessionStorage).
risk: Single-storage write would lose jury access on browser restart or cross-tab.

scenario: setJuryAccessGrant does not write when grant has no period_id
whyItMatters: Invalid grants must not overwrite a valid grant in storage.
risk: Writing an invalid grant would corrupt the jury session resume state.

scenario: saveJurySession then getJurySession returns the original session data
whyItMatters: Phone lock → browser reopen → session resume depends on this round-trip.
risk: Any field drop would resume the jury session at step 0 or with wrong identity.

story: getJurySession — returns null when token missing
scenario: getJurySession returns null when token or jurorId or periodId is absent
whyItMatters: Partial sessions must not trigger resume flow; null signals a fresh start.
risk: Returning a partial session object would resume with undefined IDs and cause API errors.

scenario: clearJurySession removes all jury session keys from localStorage and sessionStorage
whyItMatters: Logout and session end must wipe all jury state.
risk: Partial clear would resume a stale session after logout.

scenario: getJuryAccess returns null when storage throws (Safari private mode)
whyItMatters: iOS Safari private mode makes localStorage throw; the app must degrade gracefully.
risk: Uncaught throw would crash the jury entry page for all iOS private mode users.

scenario: setRawToken writes to both storages; getRawToken reads it; clearRawToken removes it
whyItMatters: Entry tokens must persist across page reloads but be per-tab isolated.
risk: Broken token storage would require admins to regenerate tokens after every page reload.

scenario: setCriteriaScratch writes to sessionStorage; getCriteriaScratch reads it back
whyItMatters: Unsaved criteria drafts must not survive browser restart (accidental state restore).
risk: localStorage write would restore stale criteria drafts after a crash.

scenario: setActiveOrganizationId(null) removes the key from localStorage
whyItMatters: Super-admin switching back to no-org context must clear the persisted org.
risk: Leaving a stale org ID would auto-select a deactivated org on next login.

scenario: useIsMobile returns a boolean reflecting window.innerWidth < 768
whyItMatters: Responsive layouts depend on this hook for conditional rendering.
risk: Non-boolean return would break strict equality checks in components.

scenario: returns sequential pages array when totalPages <= paginationItemsToDisplay
whyItMatters: Admin tables with few pages must show all page numbers without ellipsis.
risk: Incorrect page array would render wrong number of buttons.

scenario: returns correct page window when currentPage is near the last page
whyItMatters: End-of-list navigation must show the correct page range.
risk: Off-by-one error would hide the last page button.

scenario: showLeftEllipsis and showRightEllipsis are true when pages are truncated on both sides
whyItMatters: Ellipsis indicators signal that pages are hidden; wrong flags hide that context.
risk: Missing ellipsis would leave users confused about how many pages exist.

scenario: hook returns an object with a scopeRef property
whyItMatters: Components must attach scopeRef to their container to enable selection.
risk: Missing ref would silently disable all card selection behaviour.

scenario: clicking a card adds is-selected; clicking another card moves selection
whyItMatters: Single-selection model for mobile admin cards is a core UX requirement.
risk: Broken selection would leave multiple cards highlighted simultaneously.

scenario: pointer events on .row-inline-control elements do not trigger selection
whyItMatters: Comment toggles and nav badges inside cards must not activate the selection model.
risk: Missing skip would deselect current card when user taps an inline control.

scenario: vera-btn-shake class is added when error transitions from false to true
whyItMatters: Shake animation provides tactile feedback for failed form submissions.
risk: Missing animation would leave users without visual confirmation of the error.

story: useShakeOnError — no re-shake on same error
scenario: vera-btn-shake is not re-added when error remains true across re-renders
whyItMatters: Continuous re-animation would be distracting during repeated failed attempts.
risk: Re-triggering on every render would cause the button to shake incessantly.

scenario: pressing Escape key triggers the onClose callback
whyItMatters: Keyboard dismissal of dialogs and drawers is a core accessibility requirement.
risk: Missing Escape handler would trap keyboard users inside dialogs.

scenario: hook returns an object with a containerRef
whyItMatters: Components attach containerRef to the trap element; missing it disables the trap.
risk: Null ref would silently disable focus trapping in all dialogs and drawers.

scenario: calling toast.success() emits a toast with type 'success' to toastStore
whyItMatters: Toast notifications are the primary feedback mechanism for async operations.
risk: Broken emit would silently swallow all user-facing success and error messages.

scenario: messages without trailing punctuation get a period appended
whyItMatters: Toast messages must end with a period for consistent UX copy style.
risk: Missing period would create visually inconsistent notification messages.

scenario: floatingStyle.position equals 'fixed' before positioning
whyItMatters: All floating elements (dropdowns, tooltips) require fixed positioning to escape overflow containers.
risk: Non-fixed position would cause tooltips to be clipped by table overflow:hidden.

scenario: hook returns triggerRef and panelRef
whyItMatters: Row popovers attach triggerRef to the button and panelRef to the panel for positioning.
risk: Missing refs would prevent popover from computing its placement.

scenario: renders AlertCircle icon for variant='danger'
whyItMatters: Visual icon reinforces the severity level of the alert to the user.
risk: Wrong icon would mismatch danger/warning/info/success visual language.

scenario: title prop is rendered in .fb-alert-title when provided
whyItMatters: Alert titles provide the primary scannable context for the user.
risk: Missing title render would display alerts without a heading.

scenario: children are rendered in .fb-alert-desc
whyItMatters: Alert body text carries the actionable detail for the user.
risk: Missing children render would display empty alert boxes.

scenario: root element has class fba-{variant} matching the variant prop
whyItMatters: CSS classes drive variant-specific colors and border styles.
risk: Wrong class would render all alerts with the same styling regardless of variant.

scenario: renders placeholder text when no value is selected
whyItMatters: Placeholder guides the user to make a selection.
risk: Missing placeholder would show an empty button, making the field invisible.

scenario: renders the label of the selected option when value matches
whyItMatters: Users must see what they selected after choosing an option.
risk: Broken label display would show stale or empty text in the trigger button.

scenario: clicking the trigger button opens the dropdown portal
whyItMatters: Users must be able to open the dropdown to select an option.
risk: Broken toggle would prevent selection entirely.

scenario: mousedown on an option calls onChange with that option's value
whyItMatters: Options use onMouseDown to prevent blur-before-close; onClick would miss the event.
risk: Click-only binding would mean options never trigger onChange in practice.

scenario: trigger button is disabled when disabled prop is true
whyItMatters: Disabled selects must not be interactive (e.g. locked criteria weights).
risk: Missing disabled prop would allow users to change locked fields.

scenario: renders nothing when open=false
whyItMatters: Closed dialogs must not occupy DOM space or receive pointer events.
risk: Rendering when closed would block pointer events over the entire page.

scenario: title prop is visible when open=true
whyItMatters: Users need the dialog title to understand what action they are confirming.
risk: Missing title would leave the dialog without context.

scenario: clicking cancel button calls onOpenChange(false)
whyItMatters: Users must be able to dismiss a destructive dialog without proceeding.
risk: Broken cancel would force users to confirm every destructive action.

scenario: clicking confirm button calls the onConfirm callback
whyItMatters: The confirm action must trigger the destructive operation.
risk: Broken confirm button would make the dialog appear functional but do nothing.

scenario: confirm button is disabled until input matches typedConfirmation value
whyItMatters: Typed confirmation prevents accidental irreversible deletions.
risk: Bypassing the gate would let users confirm without typing the expected string.

scenario: renders an element with role='dialog' and aria-modal='true' when open
whyItMatters: Screen readers use role=dialog to announce modal boundaries.
risk: Missing ARIA role breaks accessibility for screen reader users.

scenario: clicking the overlay calls onClose
whyItMatters: Click-outside-to-close is the expected modal dismissal pattern.
risk: Missing handler would require users to find a close button to dismiss the modal.

scenario: size prop is reflected as a CSS class on the modal element
whyItMatters: Modal sizes (sm/md/lg/xl) control the maximum width of the content area.
risk: Missing size class would render all modals at the default width.

scenario: renders an element with role='dialog' and aria-modal='true'
whyItMatters: Screen readers must identify the drawer as a dialog region.
risk: Missing ARIA attributes breaks keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements.

scenario: drawer element has 'show' class when open=true
whyItMatters: The 'show' class triggers the slide-in CSS transition.
risk: Missing class would leave the drawer invisible even when logically open.

scenario: clicking the overlay calls onClose
whyItMatters: Click-outside is the primary touch-friendly way to close drawers on mobile.
risk: Missing handler would trap users in the drawer on mobile devices.

scenario: renders nothing when totalItems=0
whyItMatters: Empty lists must not show a pagination strip.
risk: Rendering pagination on an empty list wastes space and confuses users.

scenario: renders '1–10 of 50' style info text with correct values
whyItMatters: Users need to know their position in the dataset.
risk: Wrong range text would mislead users about how many items they are viewing.

scenario: first and prev buttons are disabled when on the first page
whyItMatters: Navigation buttons must be disabled at the boundary to prevent invalid requests.
risk: Enabled buttons at boundaries would call onPageChange with out-of-range page numbers.

scenario: clicking a page number calls onPageChange with that page number
whyItMatters: Page navigation is the core interaction of pagination.
risk: Broken callback would leave the table stuck on the first page.

scenario: clicking a page size button calls onPageSizeChange with that value
whyItMatters: Users must be able to control how many rows are shown per page.
risk: Broken callback would prevent users from changing the page size.

scenario: renders a button with children text
whyItMatters: FilterButton is used on all admin list pages to show filter panels.
risk: Broken render would remove filter access from admin pages.

scenario: renders a badge showing activeCount when activeCount > 0
whyItMatters: Badge tells users that active filters are affecting the list.
risk: Missing badge would hide the fact that results are filtered.

scenario: button has active class when isOpen=true
whyItMatters: Active class highlights the button while the filter panel is open.
risk: Missing class would leave the button looking inactive while the panel is open.

scenario: renders nothing when children is empty/falsy
whyItMatters: InlineError is always rendered in forms; it must be invisible when there is no error.
risk: Rendering when empty would show an empty error container below every field.

scenario: renders role='alert' element with the error message text
whyItMatters: role=alert causes screen readers to announce the error immediately on appearance.
risk: Missing role would mean visually impaired users never hear the error message.

scenario: renders nothing when toastStore has no active toasts
whyItMatters: An empty toast container must be invisible to avoid layout shifts.
risk: Rendering an empty container would create a phantom overlay region.

scenario: renders toast message text after toastStore.emit is called
whyItMatters: Toast messages are the primary async feedback mechanism in the admin panel.
risk: Broken render would silently drop all user-facing success and error notifications.

scenario: renders children directly when text prop is absent
whyItMatters: Components use PremiumTooltip conditionally; absent text must be a no-op.
risk: Wrapping without text would add unnecessary DOM nodes around every element.

scenario: tooltip text appears in document.body portal when trigger is hovered
whyItMatters: Tooltip must escape table overflow containers via portal to be visible.
risk: Non-portal tooltip would be clipped by overflow:hidden on parent table.

scenario: renders without crashing with minimal required props
whyItMatters: AlertCard is used in ConfirmDialog and throughout the admin panel.
risk: Broken AlertCard would crash every dialog that uses it.

scenario: renders initials fallback without crashing
whyItMatters: Avatars appear in juror lists and admin headers.
risk: Broken Avatar would crash any page listing jurors or users.

scenario: initials returns 2-char string; avatarGradient returns CSS gradient string
whyItMatters: Deterministic avatars must always return consistent results for the same name.
risk: Non-deterministic gradient would cause avatar color to flicker on re-render.

scenario: renders a span with level-pill class without crashing
whyItMatters: LevelPill is used in score grid and rankings tables.
risk: Broken LevelPill would crash score display across multiple admin pages.

scenario: renders fallback prop when a child component throws
whyItMatters: ErrorBoundary prevents a single broken component from crashing the entire admin panel.
risk: Broken ErrorBoundary would propagate rendering exceptions to the root level.

scenario: renders error text inside role=alert when children provided
whyItMatters: Error text must appear next to the field it describes.
risk: Missing render would show no error message for invalid field values.

scenario: renders without crashing with visible=false
whyItMatters: Loader overlay is used during async operations; must not crash when inactive.
risk: Broken overlay would prevent the loading state from working on data-heavy pages.

scenario: renders value and label without crashing
whyItMatters: StatCards populate the KPI strip on every admin list page.
risk: Broken StatCard would remove all summary metrics from admin views.

scenario: renders content inside an error variant AlertCard without crashing
whyItMatters: Blocking alerts gate form submission when validation fails.
risk: Broken component would silently allow invalid form submission.

scenario: renders children without crashing
whyItMatters: Tooltip wraps icon buttons across the admin panel.
risk: Broken Tooltip would remove all icon button tooltips from the UI.

scenario: renders without crashing when open=false
whyItMatters: ConfirmModal wraps Modal for typed confirmation; must not crash when inactive.
risk: Broken component would prevent all typed-confirmation flows from initializing.

scenario: renders children without crashing
whyItMatters: AutoGrow is used in criteria editors and comment fields.
risk: Broken AutoGrow would crash textarea-based editor components.

scenario: renders a textarea element without crashing
whyItMatters: AutoTextarea provides auto-growing textarea inputs for form fields.
risk: Broken AutoTextarea would crash all multi-line form fields.

scenario: renders without crashing in idle state
whyItMatters: Used in every async action button to show loading state.
risk: Broken AsyncButtonContent would break all async action buttons in the admin panel.

scenario: renders without crashing with required props
whyItMatters: Used in criteria and rubric editors for collapsible rows.
risk: Broken component would crash the criteria management editor.

scenario: renders without crashing with minimal props
whyItMatters: EntityMeta displays creation and update timestamps in detail panels.
risk: Broken EntityMeta would crash detail panels throughout the admin UI.

scenario: renders without crashing with an items array
whyItMatters: FloatingMenu drives all kebab menus in admin tables.
risk: Broken FloatingMenu would remove row action menus from all admin tables.

scenario: renders without crashing with empty groups array
whyItMatters: GroupedCombobox is used for project and juror multi-select fields.
risk: Broken component would crash all multi-select picker fields.

scenario: TrashIcon and TriangleAlertLucideIcon are defined exports from Icons.jsx
whyItMatters: ConfirmDialog imports these icons; undefined exports would crash every dialog.
risk: Missing export would cause a silent undefined render error in all ConfirmDialogs.

scenario: renders null when steps are empty without crashing
whyItMatters: SpotlightTour must be safe to render on any admin page.
risk: Crashing on empty steps would prevent admin panel from loading.

scenario: AuthProvider renders its children
whyItMatters: Provider must pass through children or the whole app would blank.
risk: Context misconfiguration would crash the app on startup.

scenario: When supabase returns no session, user is null and loading becomes false
whyItMatters: Unauthenticated users must reach the login screen, not be stuck on a loader.
risk: Stuck loading state would block all auth flows.

scenario: When session exists, user is populated with id and email
whyItMatters: Returning users must have their identity restored without re-login.
risk: Failed restore would force unnecessary sign-outs.

scenario: signOut clears user and organizations from context
whyItMatters: Sign-out must fully clear state to prevent data leaks between users.
risk: Residual state after sign-out would expose one user's data to another.

scenario: isSuper is true when organizations contains a super_admin role
whyItMatters: Super-admin privileges gate access to system-level features.
risk: Wrong isSuper would grant or deny admin access incorrectly.

scenario: AuthGuard renders Outlet when user is authenticated
whyItMatters: Authenticated users must reach protected admin pages.
risk: Blocking auth users would break the admin panel.

scenario: AuthGuard redirects to /login when user is null
whyItMatters: Unauthenticated access to admin routes must be blocked.
risk: Missing guard would expose admin data to anonymous users.

scenario: AuthGuard redirects to /demo (not /login) when on /demo/admin and no user
whyItMatters: Demo paths need their own redirect target to restart auto-login.
risk: Redirecting demo to /login breaks the demo auto-login flow.

scenario: useAuth returns the AuthContext value inside a provider
whyItMatters: All components rely on useAuth to access the auth state.
risk: Hook reading wrong context would silently produce undefined state.

scenario: useAuth throws an error when used outside AuthProvider
whyItMatters: Error boundary on missing provider catches misconfiguration early.
risk: Silent null context would cause cryptic downstream errors.

scenario: LoginScreen renders email input, password input, and sign-in button
whyItMatters: The login form must be reachable and complete.
risk: Missing fields would prevent users from signing in.

scenario: Filling email+password and submitting calls signIn with the credentials
whyItMatters: Form must wire inputs to the auth handler correctly.
risk: Wrong payload would cause auth failures in production.

scenario: When signIn rejects, an FbAlert with the error message appears
whyItMatters: Users must see clear feedback when login fails.
risk: Silent failures would leave users confused and unable to sign in.

scenario: RegisterScreen renders name, email, password, and org-name fields
whyItMatters: All required fields must be present for a new user to register.
risk: Missing fields would prevent account creation.

scenario: Filling valid data and submitting calls signUp with the correct payload
whyItMatters: Registration must pass the right fields to the API.
risk: Wrong payload would silently create malformed accounts.

scenario: When signUp rejects, FbAlert shows the normalized error message
whyItMatters: Users must understand why registration failed.
risk: Hidden errors cause abandonment.

scenario: ForgotPasswordScreen renders an email field and a send-link button
whyItMatters: The password reset form must be accessible.
risk: Missing form blocks the forgot-password flow entirely.

scenario: Valid email submitted → resetPassword called → sent confirmation shown
whyItMatters: Reset flow must call the API and confirm success to the user.
risk: No feedback after submit leaves users uncertain.

scenario: When resetPassword rejects, FbAlert shows an error message
whyItMatters: API failures must surface clearly.
risk: Silent failure hides reset link delivery problems.

scenario: ResetPasswordScreen renders a new-password input and submit button
whyItMatters: Password reset form must be reachable after clicking the email link.
risk: Broken form leaves users locked out.

scenario: Strong password submitted → updatePassword called → done state shown
whyItMatters: Password update must call the API and confirm success.
risk: No done-state feedback confuses users.

scenario: When updatePassword rejects, FbAlert shows the error
whyItMatters: Failed password updates must surface.
risk: Silent error would leave users with an unchanged password.

scenario: CompleteProfileScreen renders full-name and org-name fields
whyItMatters: OAuth users need this form to finish account setup.
risk: Missing form breaks the Google OAuth registration flow.

scenario: Valid name+org submitted → onComplete called with correct payload
whyItMatters: Profile completion must pass the right data to the auth context.
risk: Wrong payload leaves the profile incomplete.

scenario: When onComplete rejects, FbAlert shows the error
whyItMatters: Failures during profile completion must be communicated.
risk: Silent errors leave accounts in a broken state.

scenario: VerifyEmailScreen with no token shows error state immediately
whyItMatters: Missing token is a common deep-link failure case.
risk: Unhandled missing token crashes or hangs the screen.

scenario: Valid token in search params → confirmEmailVerification called → success state
whyItMatters: Verification link must confirm the email and show success.
risk: Broken verification leaves users unable to access the platform.

scenario: When confirmEmailVerification rejects, error state is shown
whyItMatters: Invalid or expired tokens must surface a clear error.
risk: Blank screen on failure confuses users.

scenario: PendingReviewScreen renders waiting UI and calls getMyJoinRequests on mount
whyItMatters: Users with pending applications must see status feedback.
risk: Blank screen during review makes users think registration failed.

scenario: getMyJoinRequests resolves with data → request org name shown
whyItMatters: Users must see which org their request is under.
risk: Missing org name makes the waiting state opaque.

scenario: getMyJoinRequests rejects → component renders without crashing
whyItMatters: API failures during the wait page must not crash the UI.
risk: Crash here would force a full-page error during an already-uncertain state.

scenario: GraceLockScreen renders the warning title and resend-email button
whyItMatters: Pending-deletion users need to see the warning and resend option.
risk: Broken grace-lock screen causes silent account deletion.

scenario: Resend clicked → sendEmailVerification called → sent confirmation shown
whyItMatters: Resend must call the API and confirm delivery.
risk: No feedback after resend leaves users uncertain.

scenario: When sendEmailVerification rejects, FbAlert shows the error
whyItMatters: Failed resends must surface so users can retry or seek help.
risk: Silent failure hides a broken resend path.

scenario: InviteAcceptScreen renders password and confirm-password fields
whyItMatters: Invited users need to set a password to activate their account.
risk: Missing form blocks all invite-based onboarding.

scenario: Valid matching passwords submitted → supabase.auth.updateUser called
whyItMatters: Invite acceptance must update the user password via Auth API.
risk: Wrong API call leaves the invited account without a password.

scenario: When updateUser rejects, FbAlert shows the error
whyItMatters: Failed invite acceptance must surface clearly.
risk: Silent failure leaves invited users with an unusable account.

scenario: isScoreFilled returns false for empty/null/undefined and true for 0
whyItMatters: 0 is a valid score — must not be treated as empty.
risk: Treating 0 as unfilled would prevent submission for 0-scored criteria.

scenario: isScoreFilled returns true for any finite number within scoring range
whyItMatters: All valid numeric scores must be recognized as filled.
risk: False negatives block submission for valid in-range scores.

scenario: Wrong PIN → pinErrorCode='invalid' and pinAttemptsLeft decremented
whyItMatters: Users must see remaining attempts to know when they will be locked out.
risk: Missing feedback causes repeated blind failures leading to lockout.

scenario: error_code='locked' response → pinErrorCode='locked' and pinAttemptsLeft=0
whyItMatters: Locked jurors must not see an incorrect remaining-attempts count.
risk: Wrong count misleads jurors into retrying a definitively locked account.

scenario: 2 failed attempts → pinAttemptsLeft = MAX − 2
whyItMatters: Attempt counter must track failed attempts precisely.
risk: Off-by-one would mislead users about remaining tries.

scenario: Project with one criterion filled — confirmingSubmit stays false
whyItMatters: Submission dialog must not open until all criteria are filled.
risk: Premature confirmation allows partial-score submission.

scenario: All projects filled + handleRequestSubmit → confirmingSubmit becomes true
whyItMatters: Confirmation dialog must open only after explicit submit action.
risk: Auto-opening confirmation would surprise jurors mid-eval.

scenario: handleEditScores preloads scores, comments, groupSynced from done state → step='eval', editMode=true
whyItMatters: Edit mode must restore exact prior scores so juror can adjust them.
risk: Missing preload loses prior scores, forcing re-entry of all criteria.

scenario: Navigating to next group must not mutate previous group's scores
whyItMatters: Group navigation must be non-destructive to already-entered scores.
risk: Score mutation would corrupt evaluation data without juror notice.

scenario: Fully-scored juror resuming via PIN does NOT see submit confirmation until explicit click
whyItMatters: Resuming must not accidentally trigger submission dialog.
risk: Auto-confirm on resume would finalize scores without juror intent.

scenario: useJuryState initial step is 'arrival'
whyItMatters: Jury flow must start at the arrival screen, not identity or eval.
risk: Wrong initial step skips the QR lock-in animation and context.

scenario: handleIdentitySubmit with no active periods → stays at identity with error
whyItMatters: Jurors must get a clear message when no period is available.
risk: Blank screen or infinite spinner without an error message.

scenario: PIN lockout (error_code='locked') → workflow.step transitions to 'locked'
whyItMatters: Security requirement: locked jurors must not access the evaluation.
risk: Staying at pin step exposes partial scoring UI to a locked account.

scenario: sessionExpired flag → step reverts to 'pin' with session expired error message
whyItMatters: Expired sessions must block further scoring writes and re-authenticate the juror.
risk: Allowing writes with an expired session token will fail silently at the DB layer.

scenario: writeGroup skips the RPC when score data has not changed since last write
whyItMatters: Redundant upserts under load waste DB resources and can cause lock contention.
risk: Without dedup every blur event fires an RPC even when nothing changed.

scenario: writeGroup calls upsertScore and sets saveStatus to 'saved' on success
whyItMatters: Save-status indicator gives jurors confidence that scores are persisted.
risk: Wrong status misleads jurors into thinking data is unsaved.

scenario: writeGroup with period_locked error → setEditLockActive(true) and saveStatus='error'
whyItMatters: Period lock must immediately block further writes so data is not corrupted.
risk: Continuing to write against a locked period overwrites final submission data.

scenario: writeGroup with session expired error → sessionExpired=true and editLockActive=true
whyItMatters: Session expiry must be flagged distinctly so the UI can show a re-auth prompt.
risk: Generic error hides session expiry, leaving jurors confused about lost progress.

scenario: JuryGuard redirects to /eval when no jury access grant is stored
whyItMatters: Jury routes must not be accessible without a valid entry-token grant.
risk: Missing redirect exposes score UI to unauthenticated users.

scenario: JuryGuard renders child route when jury access grant is present
whyItMatters: Valid jurors must be able to access the evaluation flow.
risk: Over-aggressive guard blocks legitimate juror access.

scenario: JuryGuard redirects to /demo/eval when no access on /demo/* route
whyItMatters: Demo jury routes must redirect to demo eval, not production eval.
risk: Wrong redirect breaks the demo jury entry flow.

scenario: consumeJuryPreload returns entry when set within TTL for matching periodId
whyItMatters: Preload cache eliminates duplicate API calls during the verification window.
risk: Cache miss forces a redundant network round-trip at the most latency-sensitive moment.

scenario: consumeJuryPreload returns null when entry is older than 20 seconds
whyItMatters: Stale preload data must not be used as it may reflect an outdated period state.
risk: Using stale data could load wrong projects or score state.

scenario: consumeJuryPreload returns null when stored periodId does not match expected
whyItMatters: Period mismatch must not silently return data for the wrong period.
risk: Cross-period data leak would show projects from a different evaluation period.

scenario: buildScoreSnapshot produces the same key for identical scores and comment
whyItMatters: Deduplication relies on a stable key — instability causes redundant DB writes.
risk: Non-deterministic key makes every blur trigger an upsert.

scenario: buildScoreSnapshot produces a different key when a score or comment changes
whyItMatters: Write deduplication must detect all meaningful changes.
risk: Missed change means an update is not persisted to the DB.

scenario: ArrivalStep renders with a continue button
whyItMatters: Arrival screen is the first thing a juror sees after QR scan.
risk: Render failure prevents any juror from starting evaluation.

scenario: Clicking Continue on ArrivalStep calls state.setStep('identity')
whyItMatters: Arrival screen must advance to identity without errors.
risk: Broken navigation strands jurors at the arrival screen.

scenario: IdentityStep renders name, affiliation fields and submit button
whyItMatters: Identity form is the entry point for juror authentication.
risk: Render failure blocks all juror access.

scenario: Submitting IdentityStep calls state.handleIdentitySubmit with name and affiliation
whyItMatters: Form submission must pass identity data to the auth handler.
risk: Missing call means authentication never starts.

scenario: PinStep renders four PIN digit inputs
whyItMatters: PIN entry is required for secure juror authentication.
risk: Missing inputs block PIN entry for all jurors.

scenario: PinStep renders FbAlert when state.pinError is set
whyItMatters: PIN error must be clearly visible so jurors understand why verification failed.
risk: Silent error leaves jurors guessing why they cannot proceed.

scenario: PinRevealStep renders the issued PIN and a continue button
whyItMatters: New jurors must be shown their PIN before they can continue.
risk: Missing PIN display forces jurors to contact coordinators for their PIN.

scenario: Clicking Continue on PinRevealStep calls state.handlePinRevealContinue
whyItMatters: PIN reveal must auto-submit the PIN and advance to evaluation.
risk: Missing call strands new jurors at the PIN reveal screen.

scenario: SemesterStep renders period options when state.periods is populated
whyItMatters: Multi-period orgs need the period chooser to work correctly.
risk: Empty list or render failure leaves jurors unable to select a period.

scenario: Selecting a period calls state.handlePeriodSelect with the period object
whyItMatters: Period selection must trigger the downstream juror auth and project load.
risk: Missing call leaves jurors stuck at period selection.

scenario: ProgressStep renders with a continue/start button
whyItMatters: Progress screen introduces the evaluation scope and must render without errors.
risk: Render failure blocks all jurors from reaching the evaluation form.

scenario: Clicking continue on ProgressStep calls state.handleProgressContinue
whyItMatters: ProgressStep must advance the juror to the evaluation form.
risk: Missing call strands the juror at the progress screen.

scenario: EvalStep renders project title and criteria inputs when project is present
whyItMatters: Evaluation form is the core functionality of the jury flow.
risk: Render failure means no juror can enter any score.

scenario: Entering a score value calls state.handleScore with project and criterion IDs
whyItMatters: Score input must propagate to state so scores are actually recorded.
risk: Missing handler call means all score entries are silently discarded.

scenario: DoneStep renders completion message
whyItMatters: Completion screen confirms successful submission to the juror.
risk: Render failure at the final step causes confusion after submission.

scenario: LockedStep renders locked/blocked message
whyItMatters: Locked jurors must see a clear explanation rather than a blank screen.
risk: Render failure for locked jurors causes silent access denial with no guidance.

scenario: JurorsPage renders page title without crashing
whyItMatters: A broken jurors page blocks admins from managing all jury assignments.
risk: Render failure hides all juror management functionality.

scenario: Filling in name, affiliation, email and clicking Save calls onSave with correct data
whyItMatters: Adding jurors is the primary way admins populate the jury panel.
risk: Silent failure means no jurors are ever created.

scenario: When error prop is set the drawer shows the error message
whyItMatters: Admins must see why a juror creation failed to take corrective action.
risk: Silent errors leave admins unaware of failed operations.

scenario: EditJurorDrawer pre-populates fields with the given juror data
whyItMatters: Edit form must show existing data so admins can make targeted changes.
risk: Empty form on edit forces admins to retype all juror details.

scenario: useManageJurors initializes with empty jurors array before first load
whyItMatters: Hook must expose a stable initial state so pages don't crash before data loads.
risk: Undefined initial state causes TypeError on first render.

scenario: PeriodsPage renders 'Evaluation Periods' title without crashing
whyItMatters: A broken periods page prevents admins from creating or managing evaluation cycles.
risk: Render failure blocks all period management.

scenario: Entering a unique period name and clicking Save calls onSave with correct data
whyItMatters: Creating periods is the entry point for every evaluation cycle.
risk: Failed saves mean no evaluation periods can be set up.

scenario: Entering a period name that already exists shows a validation error
whyItMatters: Duplicate period names cause confusion in reporting and juror assignment.
risk: Duplicate names silently overwrite or collide with existing periods.

scenario: Confirm button is disabled until the period name is typed correctly
whyItMatters: Closing a period is irreversible; typed confirmation prevents accidental closure.
risk: Missing guard allows accidental period closure during active evaluation.

scenario: useManagePeriods initializes with empty periodList before first load
whyItMatters: Hook must expose a stable initial state so pages don't crash before data loads.
risk: Undefined periodList causes TypeError on first render.

scenario: ProjectsPage renders 'Projects' title without crashing
whyItMatters: A broken projects page blocks admins from managing all student projects.
risk: Render failure hides all project management functionality.

scenario: Filling in project title and clicking Save calls onSave with correct data
whyItMatters: Adding projects is how admins populate the evaluation pool each period.
risk: Silent failure means projects can never be created.

scenario: Delete button is disabled until the project title is typed correctly
whyItMatters: Deleting a project removes all associated scores; typed confirmation prevents accidents.
risk: Missing guard allows accidental deletion of scored projects.

scenario: useManageProjects initializes with empty projects array before first load
whyItMatters: Hook must expose a stable initial state so pages don't crash before data loads.
risk: Undefined projects array causes TypeError on first render.

scenario: OrganizationsPage renders 'Platform Control' title for super-admin
whyItMatters: A broken organizations page blocks super-admins from managing tenants.
risk: Render failure disables all multi-tenant management.

scenario: useManageOrganizations initializes with empty organizations list before first load
whyItMatters: Hook must expose a stable initial state so OrganizationsPage doesn't crash.
risk: Undefined organizations list causes TypeError on first render.

scenario: createOrganization calls the API with correct payload and updates state
whyItMatters: Creating organizations is the root action for onboarding new tenants.
risk: Failed creation means new university tenants cannot be onboarded.

scenario: page renders with empty data and no active filters
whyItMatters: Reviews is the primary juror evaluation audit surface for admins.
risk: A broken import or context error would hide all evaluation data from admins.

story: buildScoreCols generates column definitions from criteria
scenario: returns criterion columns plus a total column with correct max label
whyItMatters: Columns drive the reviews table header; wrong columns break sorting and display.
risk: Stale column definitions would show incorrect score headers.

story: buildScoreMaxByKey maps criterion IDs to their max values
scenario: returns max per criterion key plus a total key
whyItMatters: Max-by-key drives score filter clamping; wrong maxes allow invalid filter values.
risk: Wrong max values allow invalid filter ranges, causing empty or incorrect filtered sets.

scenario: page renders with empty data and no projects
whyItMatters: Rankings is the score leaderboard; a crash here hides final evaluation results.
risk: Context or import errors would prevent admins from seeing the project ranking.

scenario: page renders with empty outcome data
whyItMatters: Analytics shows accreditation attainment; a broken page breaks MÜDEK/ABET reporting.
risk: Import or chart errors could blank the analytics panel during accreditation reviews.

scenario: hook mounts with no periods and returns empty arrays with loading=false
whyItMatters: Analytics data hook seeds the trend chart; wrong initial state causes chart flicker.
risk: Non-empty initial state would show stale trend data before the first API call.

scenario: page renders with empty jurors and groups
whyItMatters: Heatmap is the main scoring matrix view used during live evaluation days.
risk: A crash here would prevent admins from monitoring juror progress in real time.

story: useHeatmapData builds lookup from score data
scenario: hook returns a lookup object keyed by juror key with project scores nested
whyItMatters: Lookup drives every cell in the heatmap; wrong lookup means wrong cell values.
risk: A malformed lookup would show incorrect scores or blank cells during live evaluation.

scenario: page renders with empty KPI data and no jurors
whyItMatters: Overview is the admin landing page; a crash here breaks the entire admin session.
risk: Any broken import would prevent admins from reaching the admin panel.

scenario: page renders with empty audit log and no filters applied
whyItMatters: Audit log is the compliance trail; a broken page hides all admin action history.
risk: A broken audit page means compliance violations cannot be investigated.

scenario: hasAuditFilters is false on initial mount with empty search and no date range
whyItMatters: Filter active state drives the filter badge count and reset button visibility.
risk: Wrong hasAuditFilters would show a stale active-filter badge confusing admins.

scenario: drawer renders with a sample log entry and shows the action label
whyItMatters: The audit drawer is the main detail surface for investigating admin events.
risk: A broken drawer would prevent admins from drilling into suspicious audit events.

scenario: page renders with no active token and no session history
whyItMatters: Entry Control manages QR token access; a broken page prevents token generation.
risk: A broken entry control page means jurors cannot be granted evaluation access.

story: EntryTokenModal renders token URL and revoke button
scenario: modal renders with a token URL and displays it with a revoke action
whyItMatters: The token modal is how admins share and revoke QR access links.
risk: A broken modal prevents token sharing and revocation during live evaluation days.

story: RevokeTokenModal shows revoke confirmation with active session count
scenario: modal renders with activeCount=2 and shows the warning about active jurors
whyItMatters: The revoke modal warns admins before cutting off active juror sessions.
risk: A broken warning would let admins revoke tokens without realizing jurors are mid-scoring.

scenario: page renders with no locked jurors and empty policy state
whyItMatters: PIN blocking is the security mechanism for juror lockout management.
risk: A broken page prevents admins from unlocking blocked jurors during evaluation.

scenario: loadLockedJurors resolves with rows and sets lockedJurors state
whyItMatters: Hook load is the entry point for the blocked juror list; wrong data blocks unlock flow.
risk: A broken load would show stale or empty locked juror data, preventing targeted unlocks.

story: UnlockPinModal renders with juror PIN and name
scenario: modal renders with a pin value and juror name visible in the document
whyItMatters: The unlock modal is how admins see the new PIN after unlocking a juror.
risk: A broken modal means admins cannot see the new PIN and must manually look it up.

scenario: page renders export buttons without a selected period
whyItMatters: Export is the data extraction endpoint for post-evaluation XLSX reports.
risk: A broken export page means semester data cannot be extracted after evaluation closes.

scenario: page renders with no criteria and no period selected
whyItMatters: Criteria management is where admins configure evaluation weights and rubric bands.
risk: A broken page prevents all criteria setup — evaluation cannot start.

scenario: given a template with 2 criteria, rows are initialized with correct label and max values
whyItMatters: Form initialization from template is the entry point for every criteria edit session.
risk: Wrong initialization would corrupt existing criteria on first edit.

story: useCriteriaForm seeds one empty row when template is empty
scenario: given an empty template array, rows has length 1 with empty label
whyItMatters: Empty template seeding ensures the UI always has a row to start editing.
risk: Zero rows would cause a blank editor with no way to add the first criterion.

scenario: drawer renders with open=true and a criterion index
whyItMatters: Single criterion editor is the main edit entry point for every criterion.
risk: A broken drawer prevents all criterion detail edits.

story: StarterCriteriaDrawer renders template and copy-from-period options
scenario: drawer renders with open=true showing the starter options
whyItMatters: Starter criteria drawer is the flow entry for new-period criteria setup.
risk: A broken drawer forces admins to build all criteria from scratch manually.

scenario: drawer renders with open=true and an outcomes list
whyItMatters: Outcome mapping drawer is where criteria-to-outcome mappings are managed.
risk: A broken drawer prevents accreditation outcome mapping — required for MÜDEK/ABET.

scenario: page renders with no outcomes and no period selected
whyItMatters: Outcomes page manages programme outcomes for accreditation framework coverage.
risk: A broken page prevents all outcome management — accreditation tracking fails.

scenario: drawer renders with open=true and empty existing outcomes
whyItMatters: Add outcome drawer is how admins create new programme outcomes.
risk: A broken drawer means no new outcomes can be added.

story: OutcomeDetailDrawer renders outcome data when open
scenario: drawer renders with open=true and an outcome object showing code and description
whyItMatters: Outcome detail drawer shows the full outcome record and allows editing.
risk: A broken drawer prevents editing existing outcomes.

scenario: page renders with mocked auth and shows Settings heading
whyItMatters: Settings is where admins manage their profile, password, and security policy.
risk: A broken settings page prevents all profile and security updates.

story: ChangePasswordDrawer renders password fields when open
scenario: drawer renders with open=true and shows current and new password inputs
whyItMatters: Password change drawer is the security-critical password update flow.
risk: A broken drawer prevents admins from changing their password.

story: EditProfileDrawer renders profile fields when open
scenario: drawer renders with open=true and profile data showing the display name
whyItMatters: Profile edit drawer manages display name and email updates.
risk: A broken drawer prevents profile updates for all admins.

story: useProfileEdit initializes form from auth user
scenario: hook initializes displayName and email from auth user; isDirty starts false
whyItMatters: Profile edit hook manages the form state for the profile editor.
risk: Wrong initialization would overwrite user data on first save.

scenario: page renders showing the first wizard step welcome card
whyItMatters: Setup wizard is the critical first-run onboarding flow for new tenant admins.
risk: A broken wizard means new tenants cannot complete initial configuration.

story: useSetupWizard derives resume step from data
scenario: with no periods, starts at step 1; with period but no criteria, starts at step 3
whyItMatters: Correct resume-step derivation ensures the wizard doesn't regress or skip steps.
risk: Wrong step derivation could skip required configuration or loop the user.

story: useSetupWizard nextStep / prevStep / goToStep navigate correctly
scenario: nextStep increments, prevStep decrements, goToStep jumps; all clamp to 1-5
whyItMatters: Navigation is the core state machine of the wizard; wrong transitions break the flow.
risk: A broken step machine could skip mandatory steps or allow going out of bounds.

story: useSetupWizard marks steps completed based on data presence
scenario: when period, criteria, projects, jurors all present, completedSteps includes 2 3 4 5
whyItMatters: Completion ticks in the stepper guide admins through the wizard flow.
risk: Wrong completion state shows false ticks or hides progress, confusing admins.
